title: WIN-066 defense preemption: aetheric damping rescue + direction-match non-discriminating
target: WIN-066 detail_evidence — dome defender invokes 'aetheric damping' to explain why the dome geometry predicts 90–270 W/m² but the observed value is 0.34 W/m² (damped to fit by post-hoc adjustment). Also: direction-match (NH > SH) is claimed as dome success. Analyst Mode 3: add paragraph to detail_evidence: (1) direction-match is independently predicted by standard landmass/aerosol physics — neither side gets credit for the sign alone; (2) 'aetheric damping' rescue is unfalsifiable post-hoc fitting — no formula on the dome site derives 0.34 W/m² from dome parameters, so any observed value could be retroactively 'damped' to match. The dome's geometry is being asked to be specific (90–270 W/m²) and vague (whatever data shows) simultaneously.
